So the first thing I wanted to kind of talk about for all of you guys, because this has been on my mind a lot over the last month or so.
And now we're coming into New Year.

And so I've been thinking a lot about like my, my goals and my plans for next year.
I know that Joel posted something really cool in the community.
I think yesterday or today asking like, what's your goal?
How much do you want to make next year?
And I think that's a good thing to start thinking about.
And so I started thinking about that.
And what I always do, I always put like, like a dollar figure, like, okay, I don't want to make this much next year.
Right.
I write that down.
And then from there, I try to like reverse engine.
Like, what do I have to do to actually to achieve that?
Okay.
To start thinking about it.
And I remember one of my, one of my mentors, Dan Kennedy, it was Dan Kennedy.
They start talking about this concept he called the ascension.
Like, he looked at his company and he tried to get people to ascend up the ladder.
And some of you guys may have heard me talk about value ladders in the past or basically you bring someone in at like a, at a low level, free or, or really cheap.
And you try to provide value.
And if they get value from you, like, wow, this is really good.
They're going to naturally want to send up the next level.
They get good value there.
You can actually want to send to next and then keep sending up this, this value ladder.
And I got a really cool box about three days ago from one of our students named Simon Crier.
He lives down in Australia.
He's probably not on right.
It's like two in the morning over there.
So he might be on, but Simon's awesome.
And he's in my $10,000 coaching program and he voxed me.
I wish I could find the box real quick, but he voxed me the other day on, on phone in our high-end coaching program, people get to, to vox with me directly.
And he voxed me.
He said, Russell, he's in my entire goal for this year.
He said, I want to make, have one month where I net $25,000.
And the first month I do that is I'm sending that thing, that first $25,000 to you directly because I want to send up to your next level.
And I was like, that's the way like business should be where you provide so much value that people's entire goal in life is to give you more money so they can ascend up to the next level.
And so I want you guys to think about your business.
Like what does your business look like right now?
If you don't have one, this is the time to start thinking about this.
Like what do you have right now to provide value for someone?

And again, we're not charging for this.
We're trying to give you guys tons and tons of value.
And if you get value from it, you're probably going to want to naturally send up to the next thing, right?

And next week we're going to start maybe at the end of this week, we're going to start through a process of building funnels within the group and having people say, you know, like, post them up like Peter's doing so that we can walk you through this process because what happens is on a conscious and more importantly on a subconscious level.
When you start to build something and click funnels or anything, and you actually accomplish it, you should celebrate that like you just won the frickin gold medal, because it's an accomplishment most people like I just did some work.
No, you accomplish something and then stack on top of that.
And what happens, especially within this space is you start to realize that I can, you know, I can do this.
And that's really the message that Russell is pounding, you know, the drum about which is what one person can do another person can do if they just know what to do.
And so providing these resources for everybody, having some accountability, the ability to ask questions within the group and form relationships, which by the way, will be the relationships that you've got for the long haul.
And so you serve on them.
That's, that's the beauty of this.
And so lots to do over the holidays, no wasting time, have a lot of fun with that.

He's off the radar. radar and then he comes back and all of a sudden he's writing this copy like he's been, you know, gone through and got a doctorate degree in this particular topic.
And what I found is that, and I've, if like you suggested Robert Green's book mastery, he talks about this, it's like a Renaissance type mindset, which is he goes in and he reads all these different books from different authors and from different perspectives.
And it's not just about learning about a thing.
What Russell's been really, he implements is it starts, it's how everything connects.
It's how this connects with that.
If you read a book, you can put together some good copy, maybe sell some stuff.
But when you've got a diversified overview of it and you're able to see how all these different things relate and link together, then man, you've got a story and the ability to tell a story that is unique in the market because most people just won't do that.
And so he's talking about increasing your ability to bring in data and read it at least twice the amount of, you know, that you are currently.

And then it's like great store starter and a really crappy store manager, you know, that's, that's been my challenge and watching Russell people, you know, I work really hard at being lazy and I realize that Russell, he really works really hard to be in lazy too.
And emphasis on the work because he'll like what we talked about earlier with all the books and, uh, getting all the information and however thing links together.
The thing that I've really learned and taken away from Russell is he doesn't stop until it's done.
Right.
So I've got so many starts of businesses.
They're just, oh, they're started.
They're just left out there and they're, they never got finished.
And what I've learned from Russell is that you've got to keep the momentum.
You've got to be able to work through the boredom of the process to accomplish it because inspiration will happen when you discipline yourself to do that.
And so there's so much more to this guy than I ever really realized until you actually start to witness it.
And when I have started numerous projects that never got finished, Russell has started, you know, 50 of them that are out there in the world.
And that's one of the greatest things I can really emphasize to myself whenever I talk, I'm really teaching myself is when you start something, make sure that you're committed to getting it done.
And literally what Russell said, he made a million bucks in three weeks with the perfect webinar.
I watched that stuff happen, you know, webinars, you know, back to back, you know, it was, it was awesome.
And then from stage goes 46% of the room from stage.
So it's not just on a webinar.
What I realized, you know, what I also witnessed was two weeks prior to that Russell spent, stayed up Saturday, he stayed up Sunday, right, to finish this stuff, worked all weekend, came in on Tuesday or Monday or Tuesday, stayed till 2am, both nights, and then ran those webinars.
And what I really had to ask myself is, am I willing to do that?
You know, do I have that type of work ethic?
And what Russell is so good about is like, dude, you have to have the small wins.
That's why you celebrate, you finished your opt-in funnel.
Because when you believe that the work you're doing is going to produce the results that you hope for, then it's easy to do that.
And so that's the process with this community is to wherever you're at, let's take you to that next level and then celebrate, do some break dance and grab some dunes.
And so that's a, I think, really important for people to understand that this is not something because you're an entrepreneur, you get to be lazy and go sit my ties on the beach.
That's later.
Eventually you get there.
Eventually, right?
Right now you work.
